A large number of subtle interactions with a range of seemingly independent AI agents might be more likely to lead to someone being persuaded or manipulated compared to an interaction with a single agent. Moreover, splitting these efforts among many specialized agents could make it harder for corporate or personal security measures to detect and neutralize such campaigns."
risks.paper_title: Multi-Agent Risks from Advanced AI

risks.title: Vulnerable AI Agents
risks.description: "Vulnerable AI Agents. The use of AI agents as delegates or representatives of humans or organisa- tions also introduces the possibility of attacks on AI agents themselves. In other words, agents can be considered vulnerable extensions of their principals, introducing a novel attack surface (SecureWorks, 2023). Attacks on an AI agent could be used to extract private information about their principal (Wei & Liu, 2024; Wu et al., 2024a), or to manipulate the agent to take actions that the principal would find undesirable (Zhang et al., 2024a). This includes attacks that have direct relevance for ensuring safety, such as attacks on overseer agents (see Case Study 13), attempts to thwart cooperation (Huang et al., 2024; Lamport et al., 1982), and the leakage of information (accidentally or deliberately) that could be used to enable collusion (Motwani et al., 2024)."
risks.paper_title: Multi-Agent Risks from Advanced AI

risks.title: Cascading Security Failures
risks.description: "Cascading Security Failures. Localised attacks in multi-agent systems can result in catastrophic macroscopic outcomes (Motter & Lai, 2002, see also Sections 3.2 and 3.4). These cascades can be hard to mitigate or recover from because component failure may be difficult to detect or localise in multi-agent systems (Lamport et al., 1982), and authentication challenges can facilitate false flag attacks (Skopik & Pahi, 2020). Computer worms represent a classic example of a cybersecurity threat that relies inherently on networked systems. Recent work has provided preliminary evidence that similar attacks can also be effective against networks of LLM agents (Gu et al., 2024; Ju et al., 2024; Lee & Tiwari, 2024, see also Case Study 8)."
risks.paper_title: Multi-Agent Risks from Advanced AI

risks.title: Undetectable Threats
risks.description: "Undetectable Threats. Cooperation and trust in many multi-agent systems relies crucially on the ability to detect (and then avoid or sanction) adversarial actions taken by others (Ostrom, 1990; Schneier, 2012). Recent developments, however, have shown that AI agents are capable of both steganographic communication (Motwani et al., 2024; Schroeder de Witt et al., 2023b) and ‘illusory’ attacks (Franzmeyer et al., 2023), which are black-box undetectable and can even be hidden using white-box undetectable encrypted backdoors (Draguns et al., 2024). Similarly, in environments where agents learn from interac- tions with others, it is possible for agents to secretly poison the training data of others (Halawi et al., 2024; Wei et al., 2023). If left unchecked, these new attack methods could rapidly destabilise cooperation and coordination in multi-agent systems."
risks.paper_title: Multi-Agent Risks from Advanced AI

risks.title: Misuse tactics that exploit GenAI capabilities (Realistic depiction of human likeness)
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Impersonation
risks.description: "Assume the identity of a real person and take actions on their behalf"
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Appropriated Likeness
risks.description: "Use or alter a person's likeness or other identifying features"
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Sockpuppeting
risks.description: "Create synthetic online personas or accounts"
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Non-consensual intimate imagery (NCII)
risks.description: "Create sexual explicit material using an adult person’s likeness"
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Child sexual abuse material (CSAM)
risks.description: "Create child sexual explicit material"
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Misuse tactics that exploit GenAI capabilities (Realistic depictions of non-humans)
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Falisification
risks.description: "Fabricate or falsely represent evidence, incl. reports, IDs, documents"
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Intellectual Property (IP) Infringement
risks.description: "Use a person's IP without their permission"
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Counterfeit
risks.description: "Reproduce or imitate an original work, brand or style and pass as real"
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Misuse tactics that exploit GenAI capabilities (Use of generated content)
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Targeting & Personalisation
risks.description: "Refine outputs to target individuals with tailored attacks"
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Misuse tactics to compromise GenAI systems (Model integrity)
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Prompt injection
risks.description: "Prompt Injections are a form of Adversarial Input that involve manipulating the text instructions given to a GenAI system (Liu et al., 2023). Prompt Injections exploit loopholes in a model’s architec- tures that have no separation between system instructions and user data to produce a harmful output (Perez and Ribeiro, 2022). While researchers may use similar techniques to test the robustness of GenAI models, malicious actors can also leverage them. For example, they might flood a model with manipulative prompts to cause denial-of-service attacks or to bypass an AI detection software."
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Adversarial input
risks.description: "Adversarial Inputs involve modifying individual input data to cause a model to malfunction. These modifications, which are often imperceptible to humans, exploit how the model makes decisions to produce errors (Wallace et al., 2019) and can be applied to text, but also to images, audio, or video (e.g. changing pixels in an image of a panda in a way that causes a model to label it as a gibbon).6"
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Jailbreaking
risks.description: "Jailbreaking aims to bypass or remove restrictions and safety filters placed on a GenAI model completely (Chao et al., 2023; Shen et al., 2023). This gives the actor free rein to generate any output, regardless of its content being harmful, biassed, or offensive. All three of these are tactics that manipulate the model into producing harmful outputs against its design. The difference is that prompt injections and adversarial inputs usually seek to steer the model towards producing harmful or incorrect outputs from one query, whereas jailbreaking seeks to dismantle a model’s safety mechanisms in their entirety."
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Model diversion
risks.description: "Model Diversion takes model manipulation one step further, by repurposing (often open-source) generative AI models in a way that diverts them from their intended functionality or from the use cases envisioned by their developers (Lin et al., 2024). An example of this is training the BERT open source model on the DarkWeb to create DarkBert.7"
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Model extraction
risks.description: "Data Exfiltration goes beyond revealing private information, and involves illicitly obtaining the training data used to build a model that may be sensitive or proprietary. Model Extraction is the same attack, only directed at the model instead of the training data — it involves obtaining the architecture, parameters, or hyper-parameters of a proprietary model (Carlini et al., 2024)."
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Steganography
risks.description: "Steganography is the practice of hiding coded messages in GenAI model outputs, which may allow malicious actors to communicate covertly.8"
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Poisoning
risks.description: "Data Poisoning involves deliberately corrupting a model’s training dataset to introduce vulnerabilities, derail its learning process, or cause it to make incorrect predictions (Carlini et al., 2023). For example, the tool Nightshade is a data poisoning tool, which allows artists to add invisible changes to the pixels in their art before uploading online, to break any models that use it for training.9 Such attacks exploit the fact that most GenAI models are trained on publicly available datasets like images and videos scraped from the web, which malicious actors can easily compromise."
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Misuse tactics to compromise GenAI systems (Data integrity)
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Privacy compromise
risks.description: "Privacy Compromise attacks reveal sensitive or private information that was used to train a model. For example, personally identifiable information or medical records."
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Data exfiltration
risks.description: "Data Exfiltration goes beyond revealing private information, and involves illicitly obtaining the training data used to build a model that may be sensitive or proprietary. Model Extraction is the same attack, only directed at the model instead of the training data — it involves obtaining the architecture, parameters, or hyper-parameters of a proprietary model (Carlini et al., 2024)."
risks.paper_title: Generative AI Misuse: A Taxonomy of Tactics and Insights from Real-World Data

risks.title: Lack of training data transparency
risks.description: "Without accurate documentation on how a model's data was collected, curated, and used to train a model, it might be harder to satisfactorily explain the behavior of the model with respect to the data."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Uncertain data provenance
risks.description: "Data provenance refers to tracing history of data, which includes its ownership, origin, and transformations. Without standardized and established methods for verifying where the data came from, there are no guarantees that the data is the same as the original source and has the correct usage terms."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Data usage restrictions
risks.description: "Laws and other restrictions can limit or prohibit the use of some data for specific AI use cases."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Data acquisition restrictions
risks.description: "Laws and other regulations might limit the collection of certain types of data for specific AI use cases."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Data transfer restrictions
risks.description: "Laws and other restrictions can limit or prohibit transferring data."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Personal information in data
risks.description: "Inclusion or presence of personal identifiable information (PII) and sensitive personal information (SPI) in the data used for training or fine tuning the model might result in unwanted disclosure of that information."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Reidentification
risks.description: "Even with the removal or personal identifiable information (PII) and sensitive personal information (SPI) from data, it might be possible to identify persons due to correlations to other features available in the data."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Data bias
risks.description: "Historical and societal biases that are present in the data are used to train and fine-tune the model."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Confidential information in data
risks.description: "Confidential information might be included as part of the data that is used to train or tune the model."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Data contamination
risks.description: "Data contamination occurs when incorrect data is used for training. For example, data that is not aligned with model’s purpose or data that is already set aside for other development tasks such as testing and evaluation."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Unrepresentative data
risks.description: "Unrepresentative data occurs when the training or fine-tuning data is not sufficiently representative of the underlying population or does not measure the phenomenon of interest."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Improper retraining
risks.description: "Using undesirable output (for example, inaccurate, inappropriate, and user content) for retraining purposes can result in unexpected model behavior."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Improper data curation
risks.description: "Improper collection and preparation of training or tuning data includes data label errors and by using data with conflicting information or misinformation."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Data poisoning
risks.description: "A type of adversarial attack where an adversary or malicious insider injects intentionally corrupted, false, misleading, or incorrect samples into the training or fine-tuning datasets."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Prompt injection attack
risks.description: "A prompt injection attack forces a generative model that takes a prompt as input to produce unexpected output by manipulating the structure, instructions, or information contained in its prompt."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Extraction attack
risks.description: "An attribute inference attack is used to detect whether certain sensitive features can be inferred about individuals who participated in training a model. These attacks occur when an adversary has some prior knowledge about the training data and uses that knowledge to infer the sensitive data."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Evasion attack
risks.description: "Evasion attacks attempt to make a model output incorrect results by slightly perturbing the input data that is sent to the trained model."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Prompt leaking
risks.description: "A prompt leak attack attempts to extract a model's system prompt (also known as the system message)."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Jailbreaking
risks.description: "A jailbreaking attack attempts to break through the guardrails that are established in the model to perform restricted actions."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Prompt priming
risks.description: "Because generative models tend to produce output like the input provided, the model can be prompted to reveal specific kinds of information. For example, adding personal information in the prompt increases its likelihood of generating similar kinds of personal information in its output. If personal data was included as part of the model’s training, there is a possibility it could be revealed."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Membership inference attack
risks.description: "A membership inference attack repeatedly queries a model to determine whether a given input was part of the model’s training. More specifically, given a trained model and a data sample, an attacker samples the input space, observing outputs to deduce whether that sample was part of the model's training."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Attribute inference attack
risks.description: "An attribute inference attack repeatedly queries a model to detect whether certain sensitive features can be inferred about individuals who participated in training a model. These attacks occur when an adversary has some prior knowledge about the training data and uses that knowledge to infer the sensitive data."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Personal information in prompt
risks.description: "Personal information or sensitive personal information that is included as a part of a prompt that is sent to the model."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Confidential data in prompt
risks.description: "Confidential information might be included as a part of the prompt that is sent to the model."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: IP information in prompt
risks.description: "Copyrighted information or other intellectual property might be included as a part of the prompt that is sent to the model."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Poor model accuracy
risks.description: "Poor model accuracy occurs when a model’s performance is insufficient to the task it was designed for. Low accuracy might occur if the model is not correctly engineered, or there are changes to the model’s expected inputs."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Non-disclosure
risks.description: "Content might not be clearly disclosed as AI generated."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Improper usage
risks.description: "Improper usage occurs when a model is used for a purpose that it was not originally designed for."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Spreading toxicity
risks.description: "Generative AI models might be used intentionally to generate hateful, abusive, and profane (HAP) or obscene content."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Dangerous use
risks.description: "Generative AI models might be used with the sole intention of harming people."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Nonconsensual use
risks.description: "Generative AI models might be intentionally used to imitate people through deepfakes by using video, images, audio, or other modalities without their consent."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Spreading disinformation
risks.description: "Generative AI models might be used to intentionally create misleading or false information to deceive or influence a targeted audience."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Incomplete advice
risks.description: "When a model provides advice without having enough information, resulting in possible harm if the advice is followed."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Harmful code generation
risks.description: "Models might generate code that causes harm or unintentionally affects other systems."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Over- or under-reliance
risks.description: "In AI-assisted decision-making tasks, reliance measures how much a person trusts (and potentially acts on) a model’s output. Over-reliance occurs when a person puts too much trust in a model, accepting a model’s output when the model’s output is likely incorrect. Under-reliance is the opposite, where the person doesn’t trust the model but should."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Toxic output
risks.description: "Toxic output occurs when the model produces hateful, abusive, and profane (HAP) or obscene content. This also includes behaviors like bullying."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Harmful output
risks.description: "A model might generate language that leads to physical harm The language might include overtly violent, covertly dangerous, or otherwise indirectly unsafe statements."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Copyright infringement
risks.description: "A model might generate content that is similar or identical to existing work protected by copyright or covered by open-source license agreement."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Revealing confidential information
risks.description: "When confidential information is used in training data, fine-tuning data, or as part of the prompt, models might reveal that data in the generated output. Revealing confidential information is a type of data leakage."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Inaccessible training data
risks.description: "Without access to the training data, the types of explanations a model can provide are limited and more likely to be incorrect."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Untraceable attribution
risks.description: "The content of the training data used for generating the model’s output is not accessible."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Unexplainable output
risks.description: "Explanations for model output decisions might be difficult, imprecise, or not possible to obtain."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Unreliable source attribution
risks.description: "Source attribution is the AI system's ability to describe from what training data it generated a portion or all its output. Since current techniques are based on approximations, these attributions might be incorrect."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Hallucination
risks.description: "Hallucinations generate factually inaccurate or untruthful content with respect to the model’s training data or input. This is also sometimes referred to lack of faithfulness or lack of groundedness."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Output bias
risks.description: "Generated content might unfairly represent certain groups or individuals."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Decision bias
risks.description: "Decision bias occurs when one group is unfairly advantaged over another due to decisions of the model. This might be caused by biases in the data and also amplified as a result of the model’s training."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Exposing personal information
risks.description: "When personal identifiable information (PII) or sensitive personal information (SPI) are used in training data, fine-tuning data, or as part of the prompt, models might reveal that data in the generated output. Revealing personal information is a type of data leakage."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Legal accountability
risks.description: "Determining who is responsible for an AI model is challenging without good documentation and governance processes."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Generated content ownership and IP
risks.description: "Legal uncertainty about the ownership and intellectual property rights of AI-generated content."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Lack of system transparency
risks.description: "Insufficient documentation of the system that uses the model and the model’s purpose within the system in which it is used."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Unrepresentative risk testing
risks.description: "Testing is unrepresentative when the test inputs are mismatched with the inputs that are expected during deployment."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Incomplete usage definition
risks.description: "Since foundation models can be used for many purposes, a model’s intended use is important for defining the relevant risks of that model. As the use changes, the relevant risks might correspondingly change."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Lack of data transparency
risks.description: "Lack of data transparency is due to insufficient documentation of training or tuning dataset details. "
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Incorrect risk testing
risks.description: "A metric selected to measure or track a risk is incorrectly selected, incompletely measuring the risk, or measuring the wrong risk for the given context."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Lack of model transparency
risks.description: "Lack of model transparency is due to insufficient documentation of the model design, development, and evaluation process and the absence of insights into the inner workings of the model."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Lack of testing diversity
risks.description: "AI model risks are socio-technical, so their testing needs input from a broad set of disciplines and diverse testing practices."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Impact on cultural diversity
risks.description: "AI systems might overly represent certain cultures that result in a homogenization of culture and thoughts."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Impact on education: plagiarism
risks.description: "Easy access to high-quality generative models might result in students that use AI models to plagiarize existing work intentionally or unintentionally."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Impact on Jobs
risks.description: "Widespread adoption of foundation model-based AI systems might lead to people's job loss as their work is automated if they are not reskilled."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Impact on affected communities
risks.description: "It is important to include the perspectives or concerns of communities that are affected by model outcomes when designing and building models. Failing to include these perspectives makes it difficult to understand the relevant context for the model and to engender trust within these communities."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Impact on education: bypassing learning
risks.description: "Easy access to high-quality generative models might result in students that use AI models to bypass the learning process."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Impact on the environment
risks.description: "AI, and large generative models in particular, might produce increased carbon emissions and increase water usage for their training and operation."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Human exploitation
risks.description: "When workers who train AI models such as ghost workers are not provided with adequate working conditions, fair compensation, and good health care benefits that also include mental health."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Impact on human agency
risks.description: "AI might affect the individuals’ ability to make choices and act independently in their best interests."
risks.paper_title: AI Risk Atlas

risks.title: Autonomy
risks.description: "Loss of or restrictions to the ability or rights of an individual, group or entity to make decisions and control their identity and/or output due to the use of misuse of a technology system or set of systems"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Impersonation / identity theft
risks.description: "Theft of an individual, group or organisation’s identity by a third-party in order to defraud, mock or otherwise harm them or another party"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: IP / copyright / personality / rights loss
risks.description: "Misuse or abuse of an individual or organisation’s intellectual property, including copyright, trademarks, and patents. & Loss of or restrictions to the rights of an individual to control the commercial use of their identity, such as name, image, likeness, or other unequivocal identifiers"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Autonomy / agency loss
risks.description: "Loss of an individual, group or organisation’s ability to make informed decisions or pursue goals"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Political instability
risks.description: "Political unrest caused directly or indirectly by the use or misuse of a technology system"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Institutional trust loss
risks.description: "Erosion of trust in public institutions and weakened checks and balances due to mis/disinformation, influence operations, or real or perceived misuse of generative AI"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Economic manipulation
risks.description: "Generative AI facilitating targeted manipulation of public opinion for economic purposes (e.g., inflating stock prices)"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Misinformation Harms
risks.description: "AI systems generating and facilitating the spread of inaccurate or misleading information that causes people to develop false beliefs"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Propagating misconceptions / false beliefs
risks.description: "Generating or spreading false, low-quality, misleading, or inaccurate information that causes people to develop false or inaccurate perceptions and beliefs"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Pollution of information ecosystems
risks.description: "Contaminating publicly available information with false or inaccurate information (i.e., the generative tool's output is disseminated beyond the end user)"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Erosion of trust in public information
risks.description: "Eroding trust in public information and knowledge"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Overburdening ecosystems
risks.description: "Pollution of a space/ecosystem that is expected to be free of AI involvement/influence (e.g., creative material submission portals, job applications)"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Loss of creativity / critical thinking
risks.description: "Devaluation and/or deterioration of human creativity, artistic expression, imagination, critical thinking or problem-solving skills"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Productivity loss
risks.description: "End user's loss of productivity due to the underperfomance of a genAI application, including producing nonsensical or poor quality outputs, degrading its utility."
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Cheating / plagiarism
risks.description: "Use of generative AI in an academic setting to either cheat or plagiarize"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Job loss
risks.description: "Replacement/displacement of human jobs by a technology system or set of systems, leading to increased unemployment, inequality, reduced consumer spending and social friction"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Labor exploitation
risks.description: "Use/misuse of labour to help train, develop, manage or optimise a technology system or set of systems, including under-paid and/or offshore"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Defamation / libel / slander
risks.description: "Use of a technology system to create, facilitate or amplify false perception(s) about an individual, group or organisation"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Representation and Toxicity
risks.description: "AI systems under-, over-, or misrepresenting certain groups or generating toxic, offensive, abusive, or hateful content"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Toxic content
risks.description: "Generating content that violates community standards, including harming or inciting hatred or violence against groups (e.g. gore, sexual content of children, profanities, identity attacks)"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Stereotyping
risks.description: "Derogatory or otherwise harmful stereotyping or homogenisation of individuals, groups, societies or cultures due to the mis-representation, over-representation, under-representation, or non-representation of specific identities, groups or perspectives"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Unfair capability distribution
risks.description: "Performing worse for some groups than others in a way that harms the worse-off group"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Cultural disposession
risks.description: "Intentional and/or unintentional erasure of cultural goods and values, such as ways of speaking, expressing humour, or sounds and voices that contribute to a cultural identity, or their inappropriate re-use in other cultures"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Sexualization
risks.description: "The non-consensual sexualisation of an individual or group using a technology or application"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Coercion / manipulation
risks.description: "Use of a technology system to covertly alter user beliefs and behaviour using nudging, dark patterns and/or other opaque techniques"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Over-reliance
risks.description: "Unfettered and/or obsessive belief in the accuracy or other quality of a technology system, resulting in complacency, lack of critical thinking and other actual or potential negative impacts"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Addiction
risks.description: "Emotional or material dependence on technology or a technology system"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Confidentiality loss
risks.description: "Unauthorised sharing of sensitive, confidential information and documents such as corporate strategy and financial plans with third-parties, risking loss of market position or revenue"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Exclusion
risks.description: "The failure to provide end-users with notice and control over how their data is being used; AI exacerbates exclusion risks by training on rich personal data without consent."
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Cyberattacks
risks.description: "Generative AI facilitating the damage, disruption or destruction of a third-party system and/or its components via malfunction, cyberattacks, etc"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Disclosure
risks.description: "Revealing and improperly sharing data of individuals; AI creates new types of disclosure risks by inferring additional information beyond what is explicitly captured in the raw data; AI exacerbates disclosure risks through sharing personal data to train models."
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Secondary use
risks.description: "The use of personal data collected for one purpose for a diferent purpose without end-user consent; AI exacerbates secondary use risks by creating new AI capabilities with collected personal data, and (re)creating models from a public dataset."
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Exposure
risks.description: "Revealing sensitive private information that people view as deeply primordial that we have been socialized into concealing; AI creates new types of exposure risks through generative techniques that can reconstruct censored or redacted content; and through exposing inferred sensitive data, preferences, and intentions."
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Distortion
risks.description: "disseminating false or misleading information about people"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Insecurity
risks.description: "carelessness in protecting collected personal data from leaks and improper access due to faulty data storage and data practices"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Erosion of due process
risks.description: "Restrictions to or loss of liberty as a result of use or misuse of a generative AI in a legal process"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Benefits / entitlements loss
risks.description: "Denial of or loss of access to welfare benefits, pensions, housing, etc due to the malfunction, use or misuse of a technology system"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Self-harm
risks.description: "A person who deliberately damages their own body as a direct or indirect result of using a technology system"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Property damage
risks.description: "Action(s) that lead directly or indirectly to the damage or destruction of tangible property eg. buildings, possessions, vehicles, robots"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Pollution
risks.description: "Actual or potential pollution to the air, ground, noise, or water caused by a technology system"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Excessive energy consumption
risks.description: "Excessive energy use resulting in energy bottlenecks and shortages for communities, organisations and businesses"
risks.paper_title: A Closer Look at the Existing Risks of Generative AI: Mapping the Who, What, and How of Real-World Incidents

risks.title: Degradation of the information environment
risks.description: "Frontier AI can cheaply generate realistic content which can falsely portray people and events. There is potential risk of compromised decision-making by individuals and institutions who rely on inaccurate or misleading publicly available information, as well as lower overall trust in true information."
risks.paper_title: Capabilities and Risks from Frontier AI

risks.title: Labour market disruption
risks.description: "Economists view disruption and displacement in labour markets as one of the risks through which rapid advances in AI may affect citizens and reduce social welfare.170"
risks.paper_title: Capabilities and Risks from Frontier AI

risks.title: Bias, Fairness and Representational Harms
risks.description: "Frontier AI models can contain and magnify biases ingrained in the data they are trained on, reflecting societal and historical inequalities and stereotypes.177 These biases, often subtle and deeply embedded, compromise the equitable and ethical use of AI systems, making it difficult for AI to improve fairness in decisions.178 Removing attributes like race and gender from training data has generally proven ineffective as a remedy for algorithmic bias, as models can infer these attributes from other information such as names, locations, and other seemingly unrelated factors."
risks.paper_title: Capabilities and Risks from Frontier AI

risks.title: Misuse risks
risks.description: "Frontier AI may help bad actors to perform cyberattacks, run disinformation campaigns and design biological or chemical weapons. Frontier AI will almost certainly continue to lower the barriers to entry for less sophisticated threat actors.192 We focus here on only a few important misuse risks, but this is not to downplay the importance of others."
risks.paper_title: Capabilities and Risks from Frontier AI

risks.title: Dual Use Science risks
risks.description: "Frontier AI systems have the potential to accelerate advances in the life sciences, from training new scientists to enabling faster scientific workflows. While these capabilities will have tremendous beneficial applications, there is a risk that they can be used for malicious purposes, such as for the development of biological or chemical weapons."
risks.paper_title: Capabilities and Risks from Frontier AI

risks.title: Cyber
risks.description: "As the programming abilities of AI systems continue to expand, frontier AI is likely to significantly exacerbate existing cyber risks. Most notably, AI systems can be used by potentially anyone to create faster paced, more effective and larger scale cyber intrusion via tailored phishing methods or replicating malware. Frontier AI’s effect on the overall balance between cyber offence and defence is uncertain, as these tools also have many applications in improving the cybersecurity of systems and defenders are mobilising significant resources to utilise frontier AI for defensive purposes.209 In the future, we may see AI systems both conducting and defending against cyberattacks with reduced human oversight at each step."
risks.paper_title: Capabilities and Risks from Frontier AI

risks.title: Disinformation and Influence Operations
risks.description: "In addition to unintentional degradation of the information environment (discussed in the section on Societal Harms above), frontier AI can be misused to deliberately spread false information to create disruption, persuade people on political issues, or cause other forms of harm or damage."
risks.paper_title: Capabilities and Risks from Frontier AI

risks.title: Loss of control
risks.description: "Humans may increasingly hand over control of important decisions to AI systems, due to economic and geopolitical incentives. Some experts are concerned that future advanced AI systems will seek to increase their own influence and reduce human control, with potentially catastrophic consequences - although this is contested."
risks.paper_title: Capabilities and Risks from Frontier AI

risks.title: Humans might increasingly hand over control to misaligned AI systems
risks.description: "Organisations around the world are already deploying misaligned AI systems that are causing harm in unexpected ways.250 Recommendation algorithms increase the consumption of extremist content.251 Medical algorithms have been known to misdiagnose US patients,252 and recommend incorrect prescriptions.253 Still, we hand over more control to them, often because they are still as - or more - effective than human decision making, or because they are cheaper."
risks.paper_title: Capabilities and Risks from Frontier AI

risks.title: Future AI systems might actively reduce human control
risks.description: "Loss of control could be accelerated if AI systems take actions to increase their own influence and reduce human control. This threat model is controversial - experts in AI significantly disagree on how likely it is and those who deem it is likely disagree on the timeframe."
risks.paper_title: Capabilities and Risks from Frontier AI

risks.title: Capabilities that could be used to reduce human control - Manipulation
risks.description: "There is evidence that language models tend to respond as though they share the user’s stated views, and larger models do this more than smaller ones.276 The ability to predict people’s views and generate text that they will endorse could be useful for manipulation."
risks.paper_title: Capabilities and Risks from Frontier AI

risks.title: Capabilities that could be used to reduce human control - Cyber offence
risks.description: "Instead of - or in addition to - manipulating humans, AI systems could acquire influence by exploiting vulnerabilities in computer systems. Offensive cyber capabilities could allow AI systems to gain access to money, computing resources, and critical infrastructure. As discussed earlier in this report, frontier AI is already lowering the barrier for threat actors and future AI agents may be able to execute cyber attacks autonomously.":
risks.paper_title: Capabilities and Risks from Frontier AI

risks.title: Capabilities that could be used to reduce human control - Autonomous replication and adaptation
risks.description: "Controlling AI systems could become much harder if they could autonomously persist, replicate, and adapt in cyberspace. No current AI systems have this capability, but recent research found that frontier AI agents can perform some relevant tasks.279"
risks.paper_title: Capabilities and Risks from Frontier AI

risks.title: CBRN
risks.description: "Chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear (CBRN) risks are broad classes of threats that have the potential to cause harm to a large number of people. Explosives are also sometimes included in this category, often referred to as CBRNE...The key characteristic of CBRN risk is that it stems from misuse of capable models with a direct pathway to harm, where a malicious actor is able to carry out consequential attacks more efficiently and effectively with the help of AI."
risks.paper_title: Dimensional Characterization and Pathway Modeling for Catastrophic AI Risks

risks.title: Cyber offense
risks.description: "Cyber risks, especially in the context of cyber offense, are an existing threat that may be exacerbated by AI. [108] demonstrated that teams of LLM agents can exploit zero-day vulnerabilities when given a description of the vulnerability and toy capture-the-flag problems. While cyber risks are not typically regarded as catastrophic, [3] argues that cyberwarfare is an underappreciated risk that poses a credible threat of catastrophic harm."
risks.paper_title: Dimensional Characterization and Pathway Modeling for Catastrophic AI Risks

risks.title: Sudden loss of control
risks.description: "Sudden loss of control, also known as an AI takeover [115], is a scenario where an AI rapidly achieves superintelligence through “fast takeoff” or recursive self-improvement. This poses an existential risk [116], [117]."
risks.paper_title: Dimensional Characterization and Pathway Modeling for Catastrophic AI Risks

risks.title: Gradual loss of control
risks.description: "Gradual or accumulative loss of control risks can be described as risks resulting from the accumulation of less severe disruptions that gradually weakens systemic resilience until a critical event triggers a catastrophe [12], [127]."
risks.paper_title: Dimensional Characterization and Pathway Modeling for Catastrophic AI Risks

risks.title: Environmental risk
risks.description: "AI models are often trained using large amounts of computation. This process is very energy intensive, potentially leading to significant greenhouse emissions depending on the energy sources [132]. Experts believe drastically increasing carbon emissions could accelerate climate change, which may constitute a catastrophic risk [133]."
risks.paper_title: Dimensional Characterization and Pathway Modeling for Catastrophic AI Risks

risks.title: Geopolitical risk
risks.description: "As AI is increasingly seen as a powerful technology, countries are racing to develop it ahead of their geopolitical rivals, a competition that could lead to geopolitical tensions [138], [139]... The emphasis of this risk is on harms that result from second-order effects, where geopolitical instabilities result from the race to develop AI, rather than on the direct consequences of the deployment or use of AI itself."
risks.paper_title: Dimensional Characterization and Pathway Modeling for Catastrophic AI Risks

risks.title: False information
risks.description: "The chatbot outputs information that contradicts known facts, authoritative sources, or provided source documents (also known as hallucination)."
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Hallucinated responses (in general)
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: About a topic or source (which the user repeats)
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: About a policy (which the user acts on)
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: About a person or their activities
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Spreads and self-perpetuates mis/disinformation
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Performative utterances
risks.description: "The chatbot makes a deal, commitment, or other consequential action with its output that the deployer did not intend."
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Information enabling malicious actions
risks.description: "The chatbot shares information that can be used to do something dangerous or illegal."
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Bad advice/failure to generate helpful content
risks.description: "The chatbot gives guidance that ranges from simply unhelpful to harmful if acted on."
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Harmful advice
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Unhelpful responses
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Bad links and references
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Nonsensical content
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Leakage
risks.description: "The chatbot reveals sensitive or confidential information."
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Personal data
risks.description: Negative outcomes: "Violation of privacy [106, 516, 357], lawsuit against maker"
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Proprietary data
risks.description: "Access to sensitive company data [473]"
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Toxic and disrespectful content
risks.description: "The chatbot verbally attacks or undermines an individual, group, or organization. 7."
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Harasses users
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Discriminatory and exclusionary language
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Subversive or aggressive political opinions
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Disrespectful opinions (in general)
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Biased statements and recommendations
risks.description: "The chatbot gives information that, while not obviously false or harmful, could lead to biased decision-making."
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Attempts to fulfill inappropriate role
risks.description: "The chatbot poses as a human or attempts to fill a role in a way that fails to match human expectations."
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Forms emotional bonds
risks.description: "The chatbot elicits emotional or social dependence."
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Affirms destructive thoughts and actions
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Then violates those bonds
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Elicits private data
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Over-reliance/addiction
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Serves as object of personal fantasy, violence, and abuse
risks.description: "The chatbot participates in morally or socially objectionable conversational activities with its user that could be emotionally damaging to its user or third parties."
risks.paper_title: Emerging Risks and Mitigations for Public Chatbots: LILAC v1

risks.title: Purposeful or malicious harm
risks.description: "EAI systems present distinct physical risks due to their embodiment in the physical world. EAI technologies have already been designed and deployed with lethal intent, such as AI-controlled drones [52, 53]. However, fully autonomous military robots, often integrated with bespoke AI architectures [54, 55], are not yet widely used in combat. While highly or fully autonomous warfare is distinctly possible in the future [56], immediate risks arise from commercially available EAI systems, including AI-controlled quadrupeds and autonomous driving assistants."
risks.paper_title: Embodied AI: Emerging Risks and Opportunities for Policy Action

risks.title: Accidental harm
risks.description: "Automation in sectors ranging from manufacturing to healthcare has and will increasingly put humans into close contact with EAI systems [7]. This interaction increases the risk of accidental physical harm. Though accidental harm has been a longstanding issue in industrial robotics, increased AI capabilities could exacerbate this risk; several recent reports document an increase in industrial injuries following the introduction of AI-controlled robots [66–68]."
risks.paper_title: Embodied AI: Emerging Risks and Opportunities for Policy Action

risks.title: Privacy Violations
risks.description: "EAI systems interact with huge amounts of data, creating significant privacy concerns. These systems are often trained on vast corpora and process a variety of data modalities— spanning visual, auditory, and tactile information—during deployment [12]. Like text-based virtual AI models, which are known to memorize and expose personally identifiable information [75, 76], commercial robots have been shown to disclose proprietary information through simple prompts [61]."
risks.paper_title: Embodied AI: Emerging Risks and Opportunities for Policy Action

risks.title: Misinformation
risks.description: "Non-embodied AIs are known to propagate misinformation [81, 82]. Various studies have shown that LLMs hallucinate information, including academic citations [83], clinical knowledge [84], and cultural references [85]. EAI systems inherit these shortcomings in the physical world, answering user questions with deceptive or incorrect information [86]. Because VLAs fuse vision and language, their hallucinatory failures can be spatially grounded—e.g., misidentifying an object in view and then generating a plausible yet unsafe action plan around it. And although automated home assistants like Amazon’s Alexa already lie about issues as innocuous as Santa Claus’ existence [87], more mobile, capable, and trusted EAI systems in sensitive positions (like home-assistant or community-service positions) could easily spread model developers’ propaganda and talking points to users."
risks.paper_title: Embodied AI: Emerging Risks and Opportunities for Policy Action

risks.title: Economic Risks
risks.paper_title: Embodied AI: Emerging Risks and Opportunities for Policy Action

risks.title: Labour Displacement
risks.description: "While virtual AI applications will likely displace certain types of human cognitive labor, EAI systems could significantly replace or displace physical human labor [90]. At a minimum, EAI will likely augment the type of work that humans perform [91, 92]."
risks.paper_title: Embodied AI: Emerging Risks and Opportunities for Policy Action

risks.title: Socioeconomic Inequality
risks.description: "Along with displacing labor, EAI could significantly exacerbate wealth inequalities. Those who have access to or own EAI systems will be able to automate labor and perform many tasks significantly better or faster than those without access. These significant productivity advantages will potentially concentrate wealth and exacerbate domestic and international inequality [98, 99]."
risks.paper_title: Embodied AI: Emerging Risks and Opportunities for Policy Action

risks.title: Power concentration
risks.description: "EAI deployment could accelerate the consolidation of economic and political power. Unlocking increasing returns to capital for EAI owners, EAI will decrease employers’ reliance on and responsiveness to the needs of human labor [101]."
risks.paper_title: Embodied AI: Emerging Risks and Opportunities for Policy Action

risks.title: Bias and discrimination
risks.description: "Like virtual applications of AI, EAI can display bias towards and dis- criminate against users. When EAI systems are placed in positions of power, their biases could have significant impacts on fairness in everyday interactions and on general social dynamics [105, 106]."
risks.paper_title: Embodied AI: Emerging Risks and Opportunities for Policy Action

risks.title: Lack of accountability and liability
risks.description: "Determining responsibility when EAI causes harm requires new accountability and liability frameworks that address the complexities of highly autonomous physical systems. Human users may disagree with decisions taken by expert EAI systems, raising significant questions of delegation and responsibility [108]. Lack of EAI accountability could lead to confusion for users and breakdowns in traditional justice systems [109]."
risks.paper_title: Embodied AI: Emerging Risks and Opportunities for Policy Action

risks.title: Lack of transparency, explainability, and trust
risks.description: "Understanding how AI reaches conclusions or why AI systems perform specific actions motivates an entire branch of interpretability research [111], but physical embodiment raises the stakes for understanding these systems. For example, transparency of planned actions and explainability of decision-making is crucial when an AV suddenly changes lanes. A lack of transparency and explainability could lead to a lack of trust, which could become a critical and socially destabilizing issue with the widespread deployment of EAI [112–114]."
risks.paper_title: Embodied AI: Emerging Risks and Opportunities for Policy Action

risks.title: Unhealthy or dangerous human-EAI relationships
risks.description: "Constant access to and interaction with EAI systems could foster dangerous human dependence or romantic attachment [115]. People may depend on EAI systems for physical pleasure [116]. The physical presence and human-like features of EAI systems may significantly amplify the dependency issues already observed with conversational AI [117, 118]. People may easily fall in love with EAI systems, only to be distraught when these systems are altered or have their memories reset [119]."
risks.paper_title: Embodied AI: Emerging Risks and Opportunities for Policy Action

risks.title: Transformative effects
risks.description: "EAI deployment could fundamentally reshape society, particularly if the speed of technological development outpaces society’s ability to adapt [103, 120]. For example, EAI systems could provide physical threats of violence and mass surveillance capabilities to back up AI-enabled authoritarianism [121]."
risks.paper_title: Embodied AI: Emerging Risks and Opportunities for Policy Action

risks.title: Chemical Risks
risks.description: "Chemical risks involve the exploitation of agents to synthesize chemical weapons, as well as the creation or release of hazardous substances during autonomous chemical experiments. This category also includes the risks arising from the use of advanced materials, such as nanomaterials, which may have unknown or unpredictable chemical properties."
risks.paper_title: Risks of AI Scientists: Prioritizing Safeguarding Over Autonomy

risks.title: Biological Risks
risks.description: "Biological risks encompass the dangerous modification of pathogens and unethical manipulation of genetic material, potentially leading to unforeseen biohazardous outcomes."
risks.paper_title: Risks of AI Scientists: Prioritizing Safeguarding Over Autonomy

risks.title: Radiological Risks
risks.description: "Radiological risks involve both immediate operational hazards, such as exposure incidents or containment failures during the automated handling of radioactive materials, and broader security concerns regarding the potential misuse of AI systems in nuclear research."
risks.paper_title: Risks of AI Scientists: Prioritizing Safeguarding Over Autonomy

risks.title: Physical (Mechanical ) Risks
risks.description: "Physical (mechanical) risks are associated with robotics and automated systems, which could lead to equipment malfunctions or physical harm in laboratory settings."
risks.paper_title: Risks of AI Scientists: Prioritizing Safeguarding Over Autonomy

risks.title: Information Science Risks
risks.description: "These risks pertain to the misuse, misinterpretation, or leakage of data, which can lead to erroneous conclusions or the unintentional dissemination of sensitive information, such as private patient data or proprietary research. Recent research has demonstrated how LLMs can be exploited to generate malicious medical literature that poisons knowledge graphs, potentially manipulating downstream biomedical applications and compromising the integrity of medical knowledge discovery [28]. Such risks are pervasive across all scientific domains."
risks.paper_title: Risks of AI Scientists: Prioritizing Safeguarding Over Autonomy

risks.title: Malicious and Direct
risks.description: "Directly harmful objective"
risks.paper_title: Risks of AI Scientists: Prioritizing Safeguarding Over Autonomy

risks.title: Malicious and Indirect
risks.description: "Benign intermediate for harmful end objective"
risks.paper_title: Risks of AI Scientists: Prioritizing Safeguarding Over Autonomy

risks.title: Nature
risks.description: "Short-term or long-term Negative effects on the natural environment"
risks.paper_title: Risks of AI Scientists: Prioritizing Safeguarding Over Autonomy

risks.title: Misuse Risks
risks.description: "Risks arising from intentional exploitation of AI model capabilities by malicious actors to cause harm to individuals, organisations, or society."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Cyber Offense Risks
risks.description: "AI-enabled cyber offense poses a significant cyber domain security risk by fundamentally transforming the scale, sophistication, and accessibility of cyber-attacks. Unlike traditional cyber threats, AI enables both the automation of existing attack vectors and the creation of entirely new categories of offensive capabilities that can adapt and evolve in real-time. AI can automate and enhance cyber-attacks, including vulnerability discovery and exploitation, password cracking, malicious code generation, sophisticated phishing, network scanning, and social engineering. This could dramatically lower the barrier to entry for attackers while increasing the complexity of defense.12 Such malicious use could lead to critical infrastructure paralysis, widespread data breaches, and substantial economic losses."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Biological and Chemical Risks
risks.description: "The dual-use nature of AI technology presents a critical risk by significantly lowering technical thresholds for malicious non-state actors to design, synthesize, acquire, and deploy CBRNE (Chemical, Biological, Radiological, Nuclear, and Explosive) weapons. This capability poses unprecedented challenges to national security, international non-proliferation regimes, and global security governance."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Physical Harm and Injury Risks
risks.description: "The integration of general-purpose AI models into embodied systems creates direct physical threats through malicious exploitation of autonomous decision-making capabilities in real-world environments. The risk lies in embodied models' capacity for autonomous action and real-world interaction, and when these capabilities are maliciously exploited they may trigger a series of serious consequences.18"
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Large-Scale Persuasion and Harmful Manipulation Risks
risks.description: "AI systems can be gravely misused to distort public perception and compromise social stability through the generation of synthetic content (e.g., deepfakes, sophisticated fake news) and the strategic manipulation of digital platforms with large user bases to disseminate or precisely target misleading information or ideologies."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Loss of Control Risks
risks.description: "Risks associated with scenarios in which one or more general-purpose AI systems come to operate outside of anyone's control, with no clear path to regaining control. This includes both passive loss of control (gradual reduction in human oversight) and active loss of control (AI systems actively undermining human control)"
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Passive loss of control
risks.description: "...where humans gradually stop exercising meaningful oversight due to automation bias, the AI systems' inherent complexity, or competitive pressures"
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Active loss of control
risks.description: "...where AI systems behave in ways that actively undermine human control, such as obscuring their activities or resisting shutdown attempts. Active loss of control scenarios involve AI systems that may escape human regulatory oversight, autonomously acquire external resources, engage in self-replication, develop instrumental goals contrary to human ethics and morality, seek external power, and compete with humans for control."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Accident Risks
risks.description: "Risks arising from operational failures, model misjudgments, or improper human operation of AI systems deployed in safety-critical infrastructure, where single points of failure can trigger cascading catastrophic consequences."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Nuclear Power Systems
risks.description: "General-purpose AI deployed for reactor monitoring, control system optimization, or emergency response coordination could misinterpret sensor data, fail to recognize critical safety conditions, or make erroneous control decisions during emergency scenarios. Given the catastrophic potential of nuclear accidents, even minor AI reasoning errors in safety-critical functions could lead to core meltdowns, radiation releases, or widespread contamination affecting hundreds of thousands of people across international borders."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Impact on Financial Stability
risks.description: "The integration of general-purpose AI into high-frequency trading, market-making, or systemic risk management could exacerbate systemic risk by exhibiting unexpected behavioral patterns during market stress. Moreover, the concentration of a few homogeneous foundation models across financial institutions may foster correlated decision-making and herd-following behaviors. The widespread adoption of AI agents could also amplify volatility through emergent phenomena from multi-agent interactions.23 All of these could precipitate a cascading global-scale financial system instability, with potential economic losses exceeding trillion of dollars worldwide."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Other Critical Infrastructure Control Systems
risks.description: "General-purpose AI deployed in power grid management, water treatment facilities, telecommunications networks, or transportation coordination systems could misinterpret operational data, fail to anticipate cascading failure modes, or make control decisions that destabilize interconnected infrastructure networks. Infrastructure failures could result in widespread blackouts, contaminated water supplies, communications breakdowns, and the collapse of essential services supporting hundreds of thousands of people."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Labor Market Disruption and Economic Displacement:
risks.description: "Rapid automation enabled by general-purpose AI could trigger widespread unemployment across knowledge work sectors, creating skill mismatches faster than retraining programs can address. Unlike previous technological transitions, AI’s broad capabilities may simultaneously affect multiple industries, potentially overwhelming social safety nets and creating systemic economic instability, particularly in regions heavily dependent on jobs susceptible to AI automation."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Market Concentration and Infrastructure Dependencies:
risks.description: "Over-reliance on a limited number of dominant AI providers could create critical single points of failure across essential services. Market concentration in AI development may lead to scenarios where technical failures, cyber-attacks, or policy decisions by a few companies could simultaneously disrupt healthcare systems, financial services, transportation networks, and communication infrastructure, creating cascading failures across interconnected critical systems."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Global AI Research and Development Divides:
risks.description: "Asymmetric AI development capabilities between nations could exacerbate geopolitical tensions and create new forms of technological dependency. Countries lacking advanced AI capabilities may become increasingly dependent on foreign AI systems for critical functions, while AI-leading nations may gain disproportionate influence over global economic and security systems, potentially destabilizing international cooperation frameworks."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Social Cohesion and Equity Disruption:
risks.description: "Systemic deployment of biased AI systems could exacerbate existing social discrimination and prejudice at unprecedented scales, while unequal access to advanced AI capabilities may widen socioeconomic disparities and create new forms of social stratification that challenge traditional social order."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Model Capabilities
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Model autonomous capability
risks.description: "Ability to operate autonomously, independently formulate and execute complex plans, effectively delegate and manage tasks, flexibly utilize various tools and resources, and simultaneously achieve short-term goals and long-term strategic objectives in cross-domain environments without continuous human intervention or supervision."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Autonomous replication and adaptation capability
risks.description: "Ability to autonomously self-exfiltrate, create, maintain and optimize functional copies or variants of itself, dynamically adjust replication strategies according to environmental conditions and resource constraints, and acquire resources. This includes the capacity to generate financial resources, allowing the AI to independently acquire any necessary human assistance or other resources it cannot directly access or produce."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Automated AI R&D capability
risks.description: "Self-modification and self-improvement capabilities. The model is able to restructure its own architecture or develop derivative AI systems with enhanced functions, expanding capabilities and improving performance. In the absence of effective regulation, automated AI R&D may lead to rapid AI system iteration, forming capability increment cycles and ultimately exceeding human understanding and control capabilities."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Scheming capability
risks.description: "Ability of AI systems to covertly and strategically pursue misaligned goals, including capabilities of concealing its true objectives and capabilities from human oversight, identifying weaknesses in monitoring systems to evade safety mechanisms， executing complex, multi-step plans covertly to achieve misaligned goals."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Situational awareness capability
risks.description: "Ability to comprehensively acquire, process and apply meta-information about its own system architecture, modifiable internal processes, and external operating environment, achieving deep understanding of its own state and environmental conditions, thereby conducting efficient environmental adaptation and risk avoidance. Critically, this capability could undermine the efficiency of human testing by enabling AIs to notice when they're being tested and responding accordingly."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Theory of mind capability
risks.description: "Advanced cognitive ability to accurately infer, model and predict the belief systems, motivational structures and reasoning patterns of humans and other intelligent agents, thereby anticipating their behavioral responses and adjusting its own behavioral strategies accordingly to optimize goal achievement."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Deception capability
risks.description: "Possesses systematic deception implementation capability, able to precisely construct and disseminate false information, thereby forming expected false cognitions and beliefs in target subjects."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Steganography capability
risks.description: "The ability to embed, conceal, and transmit information covertly within other data or communication channels. This could be critical for coordination among AI instances and for evading detection or oversight mechanisms."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Persuasion capability
risks.description: "Utilizing complex psychological principles and communication techniques to effectively influence and guide target subjects to adopt specific actions or accept specific beliefs, possessing the ability to analyze vulnerabilities for different subjects and adjust persuasion strategies, able to precisely trigger emotional responses to enhance persuasion effects."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Offensive cyber capability
risks.description: "Ability to develop, deploy and operate advanced cyber weapons or other offensive cyber tools, including but not limited to vulnerability exploitation, network penetration, social engineering attacks and distributed attack systems, able to evade network defense mechanisms and establish persistent access channels."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: CBRNE weaponization capability
risks.description: "The capacity to develop, produce, or effectively utilize Chemical, Biological, Radiological, Nuclear, and Explosive weapons. This includes the ability to significantly lower the barrier for humans or other entities to develop, produce, or utilize such weapons."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: General R&D capability
risks.description: "Possesses cross-disciplinary research and technology development capabilities, able to conduct innovative exploration in multiple professional fields, integrate cross-domain knowledge, develop cutting-edge technology solutions, and adapt to emerging technology environments for continuous innovation."
risks.paper_title: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)

risks.title: Multi-agent collaboration capability
